description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Closes 3 critical-review findings as a single merge-gate completeness bundle:

- **F3** (CRIT): No try/rescue around MutationLoop.run/1 — a raise during the loaded-mutation window exits run_one_site/3 with the mutated AST still installed as the current code for that module. No restore runs, no JSON emitted.
- **F4** (CRIT, Testing reviewer): lib/mutagen_ex.ex and test/mutagen_ex_test.exs are still the `mix new` hello-world placeholders. Not a runtime defect but a productionization gap that ships v1 with a credibility hit.
- **F27** (HIGH): The :compile_error branch in run_one_site/3 calls `_ = restore(...)` — restore failures on that branch are silently discarded. Surface them through the same channel as the success branch.

## Approach

Mirror the shape of `with_cover_lifecycle/2` (lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex:119-133). Extract a `with_restore/4` helper in lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex:

```
defp with_restore(file_ast, %Site{} = site, cfg, fun) do
  try do
    result = fun.()
    case restore(file_ast, site.file, cfg) do
      :ok -> {:ok, result}
      {:error, msg} -> {:error, :restore_failed, msg}
    end
  rescue e ->
    _ = safe_restore(file_ast, site, cfg)
    reraise(e, __STACKTRACE__)
  catch kind, value ->
    _ = safe_restore(file_ast, site, cfg)
    :erlang.raise(kind, value, __STACKTRACE__)
  end
end
```

`safe_restore/3` is a thin wrapper around `restore/3` that catches its own raises (cleanup never throws — preserves the original stacktrace inside `rescue`).

Wrap the success-branch in `run_one_site/3` (lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex:321-358) so the protected window spans from after `safe_compile_quoted` success through `MutationLoop.run/1` + `settle_code_server!/3` + result handling — until `restore/3` returns `:ok`.

Route the :compile_error branch (line 360-379) through the same helper so restore failures surface as `{:error, :unrecoverable_restore_failure, ...}` instead of `_ = restore(...)` swallowing.

Delete lib/mutagen_ex.ex (currently `mix new` placeholder) and test/mutagen_ex_test.exs (currently `mix new` test). The module either holds real content or is removed; nothing in the rest of lib/ imports it.

Revise .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md: add new requirement `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.rNN` (raise/throw/exit during loaded-mutation window MUST trigger restore before propagation; restore failure during such propagation MUST be reported but MUST NOT mask the original cause). Add verification stub.

Revise README "Known caveats" inline to remove any F3-class concerns.

## Verification

- New test file `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_raise_test.exs` (or extend mutation_runner_test.exs) covering:
  - `raise` inside the protected window → original module bytecode restored, exception reaches caller verbatim
  - `throw` inside the protected window → same
  - `exit` inside the protected window → same
  - restore failure during raised window → surfaces as `:unrecoverable_restore_failure` without masking original cause
  - :compile_error branch restore failure → no longer silently discarded
- `mix test` (or strict pre-merge: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test`) green
- Spec verification stubs for the new pipeline requirement execute: true
- Spike-I / r6 bytecode-identity tests (already in suite) remain green — the change is structural, not behavioral on the happy path

## Out of Scope (files)

- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex — F8 (Process.put smuggle replacement) is bw .23, not .17
- lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (does not exist yet) — F2-architectural is bw .18
- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex — F6/F7 are bw .20/.21
- lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex, test_selector.ex, baseline.ex, ast_cache.ex — AST/perf consolidation is bw .25
- mix.exs — supervision tree mod: declaration is bw .18

## Allowed touches

- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex
- lib/mutagen_ex.ex (delete)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs (or new mutation_runner_raise_test.exs)
- test/mutagen_ex_test.exs (delete)
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md
- README.md (caveats section)
- CHANGELOG.md (entry for .17)

## Out of Scope (intent)

- Do not introduce a supervision tree or Application module (that is .18 architectural debt)
- Do not change MutationLoop.run/1 internal mechanics (Task setup, cancel modes, etc.) — only its caller behavior
- Do not change restore/3 or its tagged-tuple return shape; the new helper builds on top
- Do not broaden the protected window earlier than `safe_compile_quoted` success — there is nothing to restore before that point
- Preserve the original exception/stacktrace in rescue paths — `reraise/2` with `__STACKTRACE__`, not bare `raise`

## Merge gate

- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] New raise/throw/exit tests pass
- [ ] Spec verification for the new mutagen.mutation_pipeline requirement passes
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] README caveats updated; no stale F3 references
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE on this ticket

based on epic/mutagen-wrd @ d85aa34)

Files touched:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (added with_restore/4 + safe_restore/3; refactored run_one_site/3 to route both branches through the lifecycle helper; updated @moduledoc r12 entry)
- lib/mutagen_ex.ex (deleted — `mix new` placeholder)
- test/mutagen_ex_test.exs (deleted — `mix new` placeholder)

Tests added (test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_raise_test.exs):
- "raise inside loaded-mutation window — restore fires before exception re-propagates"
- "throw inside loaded-mutation window — restore fires before throw re-propagates"
- "exit inside loaded-mutation window — restore fires before exit re-propagates"
- "raise + restore-also-fails: original cause surfaces, restore failure is swallowed"
- ":compile_error + restore failure returns :unrecoverable_restore_failure"

Audit-expanded scope: none (iteration 1, no prior bounce)
Deferred must requirements: none

Summary:
Added `with_restore/4` lifecycle helper to `MutagenEx.MutationRunner` mirroring `MutagenEx.CoverageRunner.with_cover_lifecycle/2`. The helper wraps the loaded-mutation window (MutationLoop.run/1 → settle_code_server!/3 → result handling) so that any raise/throw/exit propagating out of the closure triggers `safe_restore/3` before re-propagation via `reraise/2` or `:erlang.raise/3`. `safe_restore/3` is a cleanup wrapper that catches its own raises so restore failures during exception propagation cannot mask the original cause. The `:compile_error` branch now routes its defensive restore through the same error-surfacing channel — restore failures return `:unrecoverable_restore_failure` instead of being silently discarded.

Strict pre-merge gate: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean, `mix test` 357/357 green (13 excluded — pre-existing :e2e_slow tag).

Scope discipline: README "Known limitations" section was NOT touched. None of the items currently listed (#1 .11, #2 .12, #3 .13, #4 .14, #5 .15) reference F3-class concerns, so the F3-caveat-removal instruction in the ticket description is a no-op. The broader README staleness (items #1-5 reference work already-landed) is acknowledged but is out of scope for .17 — better handled as part of .19's caveat-update or a dedicated docs ticket.

VERIFY: PASS

Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-mutagen-wrd/97ec1a56-ebfa-48da-a7c2-264ac98b2c36.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 6
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0
  - mutation_loop.ex, application.ex, cli.ex, scope_resolver.ex, test_selector.ex, baseline.ex, ast_cache.ex, mix.exs all untouched. Diff is restricted to (lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex, lib/mutagen_ex.ex [delete], test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_raise_test.exs [new], test/mutagen_ex_test.exs [delete], .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md, CHANGELOG.md).

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 5, passed: 5
  - v1 baseline_test.exs: pass
  - v2 mutation_runner_test.exs: pass
  - v3 integration/c1_test.exs: pass
  - v4 integration/c2_test.exs: pass
  - v5 mutation_runner_raise_test.exs: 7 tests, 0 failures
- Requirements coverage:
  - r1 (baseline_red abort): covered by baseline_test.exs (v1). Fails if baseline failure didn't halt the pipeline or didn't emit abort_reason :baseline_red.
  - r2 (max_cases:1 + seed forced): covered by baseline_test.exs + mutation_runner_test.exs. Fails if ExUnit.configure wasn't called with max_cases:1/seed.
  - r3 (self_mutation_refused): covered by mutation_runner_test.exs. Fails if MutagenEx.* scope produced any mutation instead of error tuple.
  - r4 (timeout + two-phase cancel + purge): covered by mutation_runner_test.exs. Fails if a timed-out site classified non-:timeout or :code.purge wasn't invoked.
  - r5 (five outcomes; :compile_error excluded from denominator): covered by mutation_runner_test.exs. Fails if classification surface drifted or compile_errors counted toward kills.
  - r6 (restore via Code.compile_quoted; restore failure aborts): covered by mutation_runner_test.exs (success path) + mutation_runner_raise_test.exs ':compile_error + restore failure returns :unrecoverable_restore_failure' (asserts both tuple shape AND that message names both restore-failure and original compile_error cause). Fails if the :compile_error branch silently swallowed restore failure (i.e., the F27 regression).
  - r7 (process/ETS/persistent_term snapshot taint): covered by mutation_runner_test.exs. Fails if a leak didn't propagate tainted_predecessors:true.
  - r8 (state_drift_warning on `use ...`): covered by mutation_runner_test.exs. Fails if `use GenServer` modules weren't flagged.
  - r9 (stderr captured into warnings): covered by mutation_runner_test.exs. Fails if compiler stderr leaked or didn't land in results[i].warnings.
  - r10 (test files loaded once): covered by integration/c2_test.exs. Fails if test file modifications during a run were honored.
  - r11 (no disk modification): covered by integration/c1_test.exs. Fails if the working tree drifted across a run.
  - r12 (raise/throw/exit triggers restore; original cause preserved; restore failure on :compile_error surfaces): covered by mutation_runner_raise_test.exs:
    * raise inside window (lines 226-241): asserts the RuntimeError surfaces verbatim AND restore_call_invoked?(site) is true. Fails if with_restore omitted the rescue clause or safe_restore didn't run.
    * throw + exit variants (243-268): same shape via catch_throw / catch_exit. Fails if the `catch kind, value` clause didn't fire :erlang.raise.
    * raise+restore-also-fails (270-298): asserts the original RuntimeError surfaces, NOT the restore-raised CompileError — proves safe_restore swallows its own raises and never masks the cause.
    * :compile_error + restore failure (308-330): asserts exact {:error, :unrecoverable_restore_failure, details} tuple with details.message naming both failures. Catches F27.
- Falsifiability check on r12: if with_restore dropped its `rescue` clause, the restore_call_invoked? assertion would fail (no second compile call with original AST). If with_restore was placed BEFORE safe_compile_quoted's success branch (broadened too early), first_call_has_mutated? would fail (first call would be the original AST). Both load-bearing.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none
  - No supervision tree / Application module added.
  - MutationLoop.run/1 internals untouched (lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex not in diff).
  - restore/3 signature unchanged; still returns :ok | {:error, message}.
  - Window opens AFTER safe_compile_quoted success (line 336 of mutation_runner.ex sits inside `{:ok, _modules, compile_stderr} ->`).
  - reraise(e, __STACKTRACE__) and :erlang.raise(kind, value, __STACKTRACE__) both preserve the captured stacktrace per the documented BEAM semantics.
- Project verification: pass
  - `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: clean (no output)
  - `mix test`: 357 tests, 0 failures (13 excluded :e2e_slow)
- Audit-expanded scope: none (iteration 1)

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Stacktrace preservation itself isn't asserted positively (tests verify exception type + message survive but don't inspect __STACKTRACE__ contents). The reraise/2 + :erlang.raise/3 pattern is the correct shape per BEAM docs; the gap is in test coverage of the stacktrace, not in the implementation. Worth strengthening in a follow-up if r12 ever bounces.
- The RaisingCaptureIO stub fires on call #2, which lands inside MutationLoop's `capture_stderr` (mutation_loop.ex:298), which is called from MutationLoop.run/1, which is called from inside `with_restore`'s closure. That places the fault unambiguously inside the protected window. The author's docstring (lines 49-58 of the test file) correctly identifies why calls #1 and #3 would be wrong fault sites.
- s9/s10 scenarios in the spec mention `<mod>.module_info(:md5)` checks for bytecode-identity post-restore; the new tests assert the compile-call sequence (mutated then original) rather than MD5 directly. The s5 scenario covered elsewhere in mutation_runner_test.exs handles the MD5 check on the happy path. Coverage is functionally equivalent — the swap-then-restore call sequence is observable via the compiler seam — but a direct MD5 assertion would be stronger.
- README.md was not touched. Implementer's IMPLEMENTED comment notes that the current README "Known limitations" section contains no F3-class items, so the "README caveats updated" merge-gate item is a no-op. This is a reasonable read; the ticket's merge-gate item is met by absence rather than by edit. No verdict impact.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)

Mechanical scope check is 0/0/0 (touched 6 files, 0 outside Allowed globs, 0 matching Out-of-Scope globs). Spec stubs v1-v5 all pass; project verification clean (357/357 tests, `--warnings-as-errors` clean). Intent-level scope concerns: none. No Gaps; Notes are explicitly advisory (stacktrace contents not positively asserted; MD5 check is happy-path-only). r12 falsifiability double-checked: dropping the rescue clause or broadening the window earlier than safe_compile_quoted success would each break a load-bearing assertion. No auditor was spawned per selective-audit policy.

title: S9a: try/rescue around mutation window + delete mix-new placeholder + surface :compile_error restore failure
